chosen_action.title: Retrofit Codeberg-first first-run CTAs onto high-intent Ralph Workflow blog posts

why_this_action.summary: The current bottleneck is conversion_to_free_use, live external lanes are already in overlapping measurement windows, and three high-intent Ralph Workflow blog posts still lacked a direct Codeberg-first evaluator path. The highest-leverage action available right now was to tighten those owned conversion surfaces instead of creating more outbound overlap.

result.summary: Added Codeberg-primary evaluator CTAs, first-task-guide links, START_HERE links, and the morning-after merge question to the three owned blog posts that still ended without a repo-directed adoption path. Also expanded the public conversion regression tests so these high-intent posts fail closed if the CTA disappears or GitHub moves ahead of Codeberg.

expected_outcome: High-intent owned content now sends evaluators to Codeberg first with a concrete first-task path, increasing the odds that existing traffic turns into primary-repo inspection and adoption signals instead of article-only consumption.

measurement_window.success_metric: By the next Apollo/repo-review checkpoint, all three retrofitted blog posts still preserve Codeberg-first CTAs and any owned-content-driven repo movement shows up on Codeberg before GitHub mirror vanity metrics.
measurement_window.kill_condition: If Codeberg adoption stays flat through the next review despite these repaired owned surfaces, treat older owned-content conversion polish as insufficient and shift the next slot to a different executable demand-capture lane or stronger proof-asset surfacing.
